allowed_files: Architecture Contract: Maintain a single Next.js 16.x TypeScript application with unified package, tsconfig, and Next config for shared UI/API code.
Backend Contract: Provide Node 24-ready Next.js config, better-sqlite3 seeding hooks, and puzzle definitions consumable by the frontend.
Gameplay Contract: Implement Blockly workspace with fixed On Start root, warnings, telemetry hooks, text-to-speech goal option, and Pixi/WebGL scene renderer with animation + replay support.

Telemetry & Analytics Contract: Implement /api/session/*, /api/events/batch, and /api/analytics/* so better-sqlite3 captures sessions, attempts, events, movements, and exposes aggregates plus replay-ready detail feeds.
Telemetry Contract: Implement /api/session/start|end, /api/events/batch, and /api/analytics/* routes writing attempts/events/movements plus exposing aggregates and replay feeds.
UI Contract: Deliver landing, map, puzzle, and analytics screens in pink/violet cartoon style with keyboard-friendly controls and Show Code toggle.

repair_result.root_cause: Worker filesystem policies compared created paths literally against owned_paths, so directory entries like "components" failed to cover their nested files and triggered allowlist violations.
repair_result.summary: Extended `matches_any_glob` to treat plain owned paths as directory prefixes, letting entries such as `components` authorize files beneath them and preventing false policy violations on reruns (`orchestrator.py:165`).
verification: /usr/bin/python3 - <<'PY' …  # exercised matches_any_glob on directory and file cases
